Flagship venture

Phansora — my AI product suite

Beyond client work, I build and run my own AI products. Phansora is a suite of four: Book Alchemy, Dossier Nova, Chrono Origin, and SpokenVerse — proof I ship real, self-serve AI, not just slide decks.

How I work

A simple, honest process

01

Discover

We pin down the real problem and whether AI is the right tool.

02

Design

A pragmatic plan: scope, data, architecture, and cost.

03

Build

Iterative delivery you can see and steer every week.

04

Ship & support

Deployed to production, monitored, and improved.

FAQ

Questions, answered

What kinds of AI projects do you take on?

Everything from LLM-powered apps and agents, RAG and semantic search, and document automation to predictive machine-learning models and computer vision. If it involves shipping real, production AI software, it's in scope.

Do you work with startups, or only enterprises?

Both. I've delivered for enterprises like T-Mobile and for lean startups. The engagement scales to the problem — a focused prototype for a startup or a production system for an enterprise team.

Is AI actually the right fit for my problem?

Sometimes it isn't — and I'll tell you honestly. Every engagement starts with discovery to confirm AI adds real value before any build. If a simpler tool wins, that's the recommendation.

How long does a typical project take?

A validated prototype often takes 2–4 weeks; a production-ready system typically runs 6–12 weeks depending on scope, data, and integrations. You see working software every week either way.

Can you work with our existing team and tech stack?

Yes. I integrate with your engineers, tools, and infrastructure — Python, TypeScript, React, cloud, your data warehouse — rather than forcing a rewrite.

How do we get started?

Send a short note about what you're building via the contact page. You'll get a personal reply, usually within a day, and a short call to scope it.

Let’s build something intelligent

Tell me what you’re working on — I’ll tell you honestly whether AI is the right tool, and how I’d build it.
